name: Maya
label: Non-technical operator
background: Executive assistant turned operations coordinator. Comfortable with browsers and spreadsheets, new to terminal and Git.
idea: Busy executive assistants lose track of recurring follow-ups after meetings because action items are scattered across notes, email, and Slack. I want a simple approval-based follow-up tracker that drafts reminders and shows what needs attention today.

name: Ravi
label: Product manager
background: Product manager who writes specs but has never shipped code personally.
idea: Product managers lose customer evidence because interview notes, Slack threads, and roadmap decisions live in different places. I want a research synthesis app that turns raw notes into tagged opportunities while keeping the PM in control of the final interpretation.
target_user: B2B SaaS product managers running customer discovery
likely_blockers: not wanting to touch deployment details

name: Lena
label: Designer-founder
background: UX designer with strong taste, light coding exposure, and a real audience.
idea: Freelance UX designers waste Sunday evenings turning client status notes into polished updates. I want a client update assistant that tracks deliverables and drafts weekly updates in the designer's voice for approval.
target_user: freelance UX designers with 3-8 active clients
likely_blockers: feeling the app is too ugly to share

name: Alex
label: Experienced engineer
background: Full-stack engineer who wants to learn agentic SDLC and move faster without losing quality.
idea: Platform engineers spend too much time checking dashboards and Slack after incidents to understand what changed. I want an incident briefing agent that gathers deploys, alerts, and notes into a short morning brief with links to evidence.
target_user: platform engineers on small SaaS teams

name: Nora
label: Career changer
background: Former teacher learning to build software products. High motivation, low confidence.
idea: Tutors lose time writing personalized practice notes after each session. I want a tutoring follow-up app that stores session notes and drafts practice plans that the tutor reviews before sending.
target_user: independent tutors working with middle and high school students
